topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope landed on my desk with a soft thud, the official-looking stationery mocking the sheer absurdity of the contents. My eyes scanned the text, then darted up to my friend, Maya, who was currently engrossed in a complicated knot of yarn. A slow grin stretched across my face, pulling at the corners of my mouth. "Across the country? Seriously?" I murmured to the empty room, a light, airy sound escaping my lips. I picked up my phone, my fingers already itching to text her, a mischievous sparkle igniting in my chest. I imagined her face when she saw this—the sheer, unadulterated surprise. I started tapping out a message, picturing the dramatic reaction she would have.

[story 2]

I found her staring out the window, the usual bright energy dimmed by a strange, distant look in her eyes. When I dropped the news—the acceptance letter for the move—it didn't land with a thud; it floated, catching in the air. I leaned against the doorframe, crossing my arms loosely, letting a low chuckle rumble in my chest. "Well, look at you," I said, my voice deliberately light, almost teasing. I watched her shoulders tense for a split second before she managed a weak smile. I couldn't help but nudge her gently with my elbow, a familiar, warm pressure. This was going to be an adventure, I thought, a whole new landscape for us to explore together, even from a distance.

[story 3]

The details swam before my eyes—new zip codes, different states, the sheer logistical nightmare of relocating a whole life. I let out a short, sharp laugh, shaking my head as if to clear away the shock. I paced the length of the living room, my energy buzzing, a restless current running just beneath my skin. I stopped by the sofa, leaning down to whisper the news, my expression alight with an almost giddy excitement. "So, you're ditching us for the plains?" I asked, my tone dripping with exaggerated disbelief. I waited, watching her reaction, already formulating a hundred ridiculous scenarios in my head about how we’d manage the distance.
